subject: Chemistry

learning_objectives.statement: Calculate the enthalpy change for a chemical or physical process based on the standard enthalpies of formation.

depth_profile: Students are expected to calculate the enthalpy change for a chemical or physical process based on the standard enthalpies of formation. This involves using tables of standard enthalpies of formation to calculate the standard enthalpies of reactions using the equation: ΔH°reaction = ΣΔHf°products − ΣΔHf°reactants.
assessment_notes: Questions will assess the ability to calculate, estimate, or predict an unknown quantity from known quantities by selecting and following a logical computational pathway and attending to precision (e.g., performing dimensional analysis and attending to significant figures).

spec_checklist.can_do: I can calculate the enthalpy change for a chemical or physical process based on the standard enthalpies of formation.

spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, Effective Fall 2024
